name: Ring route from Côja towards the Serra do Açor
name_original: Giro ad anello da Côja verso la Serra do Açor
region_intro: Road cycling around Cerdeira, nestled within Portugal's Serra da Lousã mountains, offers routes characterized by significant elevation changes and rugged terrain. The region features a "permanent up and down" topography, with climbs typically ranging from 6-8% grades on generally well-maintained tarmac roads. Cyclists can navigate through dense forests, past rivers, and explore the unique Aldeias do Xisto (Schist Villages). This area provides challenging routes for experienced riders. Best road cycling routes around Cerdeira The most popular road cycling route is Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour, a 32.2 miles (51.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route features steady climbs and panoramic views towards Monte Colcurinho. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ring route from Côja towards the Serra do Açor, a difficult 50.7 miles (81.7 km) path. This route traverses the rugged terrain of Serra do Açor, known for significant elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the Côja – Castelo de Avô loop from Vale do Torno, a 42.5 miles (68.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including river valleys and historic points, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Cerdeira is defined by the Serra da Lousã mountains, schist villages, and dense forests, with routes featuring significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Cerdeira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cerdeira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling around Cerdeira?
A: Road cycling around Cerdeira is characterized by a "permanent up and down" topography within the Serra da Lousã mountains. You can expect significant elevation changes, with climbs typically ranging from 6-8% grades, and some sections reaching 10-11%. The road surfaces are generally well-maintained tarmac, winding through dense forests and rugged landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Cerdeira?
A: Yes, Cerdeira is ideal for cyclists who enjoy climbs and challenging routes. Most of the routes in the area are classified as difficult, offering long, steady ascents and exhilarating descents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2314117892/epic-climb-to-monte-colcurinho-loop-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour&lt;/a&gt; features substantial elevation gain over its 51.8 km.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Cerdeira?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are a couple of moderate routes available. These still involve elevation changes but are less demanding than the difficult options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Secarias River Beach – Côja loop from Côja e Barril de Alva&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 56.6 km route that offers a less intense experience.

Q: What scenic views can I expect on road cycling routes near Cerdeira?
A: The mountainous terrain ensures spectacular panoramic views. You'll often see vistas towards Serra da Estrela, vast forests, and valleys. Higher elevations might reveal wind farms. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2314117892/epic-climb-to-monte-colcurinho-loop-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour&lt;/a&gt; are particularly noted for their scenic outlooks. You can also find specific viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6902109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Piódão&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/400928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic View of Serra da Lousã&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I visit any unique villages or historical sites while road cycling?
A: Absolutely. Cerdeira is part of the Aldeias do Xisto (Schist Villages) network, and cycling through these traditional stone villages offers a glimpse into rich cultural heritage. Many routes pass through or near these picturesque settlements. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Côja – Historical Village of Piódão loop from Vendas de Galizes&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historical Village of Piódão&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions like waterfalls or rivers along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. Cyclists can encounter rivers such as the Arouce, São João, Cerdeira, and Candal, which often accompany various routes. You might also find natural dams where swimming is possible. A prominent natural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2856836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fraga da Pena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the river beach at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5885127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz d'Égua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cerdeira?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for road cycling in Cerdeira. The weather is typically milder, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer cold or rainy days than winter, making for more comfortable riding conditions.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Cerdeira are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2314117892/epic-climb-to-monte-colcurinho-loop-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2315558966/ring-route-from-coja-towards-the-serra-do-acor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ring route from Côja towards the Serra do Açor&lt;/a&gt;, both offering comprehensive circular experiences.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Cerdeira?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging yet rewarding climbs, the stunning panoramic views, and the unique experience of cycling through the traditional Aldeias do Xisto. The diverse natural environment, from dense forests to rivers, also contributes to a memorable ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the Cerdeira area?
A: Yes, the region offers several longer routes for endurance cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2315558966/ring-route-from-coja-towards-the-serra-do-acor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ring route from Côja towards the Serra do Açor&lt;/a&gt; spans over 81 kilometers, traversing rugged terrain with significant elevation changes, making it a substantial challenge for experienced riders.

Q: Is Cerdeira part of a larger cycling network or project?
A: Yes, Cerdeira is integrated into the "Cyclin' Portugal" project, which promotes it as a premier cycling destination. This initiative aims to provide quality infrastructure and services tailored for cyclists, including Bikotel accommodations, enhancing the overall cycling experience in the region.
tips: This road offers you breathtaking views of the surrounding mountain landscapes, characterized by dense forests, deep valleys and agricultural terraces.

Fajão is nestled between the Serras del Açor and de la Lousã, and stands out for its unique architecture, made mainly of schist (xisto), the local stone that gives it a dark color and a characteristic appearance, making it one of the famous "schist villages" of the region. 

Fajão also has a parish museum that exhibits sacred art and objects of daily life, and boasts a history linked to the Resistance during the Salazarist regime.

Nestled along the banks of the Alva River, Côja is immersed in a green environment, surrounded by mountains and forests. The village is characterized by traditional architecture, a charming Roman bridge and a peaceful atmosphere.
